content.title: From Hand to Mouth
content.summary: {'A': '南部非洲再次面临严重的粮食危机，这是一个长期存在的问题，但不应该存在；在马拉维南部恩桑杰区的三一医院，三岁的姆博伊无精打采地躺在那里，他的脸贴在墙上，肚子严重膨胀，腿上的皮肤脱落；他的母亲解释说，由于降雨不足，他们今年无法收获任何东西；该地区的母亲们已经带着营养不良的孩子前往医院的数量令人担忧，然而，下一次收获还要等六个月。', 'B': '援助机构正在发出警报，希望在西方电视屏幕上出现尼日尔等地瘦弱儿童的令人不安的形象之前能得到帮助。联合国世界粮食计划署表示，在下一次收获之前，南部非洲约有1200万人需要粮食援助，该机构缺少超过1.5亿美元的资金来满足他们在接下来的六个月内的需求。马拉维和津巴布韦是最饥饿的地区，但莫桑比克、赞比亚、莱索托和斯威士兰也受到影响。这场干旱可能是南部非洲十年来最严重的。一月份关键的降雨没有按时到来，新种植的作物需要水，而在一些地方种子和化肥也没有及时供应。玉米这种主食在一些地区供应不足，市场价格已经超出了最饥饿人群的承受能力。', 'C': '尽管今年的问题特别严重，但它在整个南非洲都是反复出现的。食物主要由没有灌溉的自给农民在小块土地上生产，他们的命运与适量适时的降雨有关。糟糕的道路和不可靠的运输使得食物和种子的运输成本高昂。没有适当的营销渠道，小农户无法将他们可能拥有的剩余物品卖到他们所在的社区之外。这导致莫桑比克南部的人们饥饿，即使北部的庄稼丰收。玉米不适合这种气候，需要太多的水。在马拉维，土地上的人口过多。部分原因是由于糟糕的农业，产量低下。而该地区拥有世界上最高的艾滋病率。', 'D': '许多小农户在好年景中也难以维持生计，所以一年的不好收成对他们来说是灾难性的。在斯威士兰和莫桑比克，他们已经连续面临了四年的干旱。无法种植足够的粮食来养活自己或借贷度过困难时期，农民最终失去了他们所拥有的少量资产。在马拉维，那些一无所有的人通常会采取砍伐和销售柴火的方式，进一步侵蚀土壤，使他们的土地产量更低，或者去捕捉已经枯竭的水域中的鱼。其他人冒险进入充满鳄鱼的河流中挖掘睡莲的块茎作为食物。', 'E': '坏的政府政策有时会使情况变得更糟。在津巴布韦，土地抢夺破坏了商业农业和灌溉系统，恶性通货膨胀和外汇短缺使得购买种子和肥料变得困难，燃料短缺阻碍了农作物的运输，而最近一次清理所谓非法住所的行动使另外70万人陷入贫困，增加了饥饿人口的数量，政府迄今拒绝支持联合国提出的帮助受灾人员的紧急计划，而其他国家政府则不那么冷酷无情，最受影响的国家马拉维希望得到帮助，总统穆塔里卡呼吁国民捐款以“养活全国”，目前已筹集到56.5万美元，联合国在8月呼吁筹集8800万美元，世界银行将提供3000万美元。', 'F': '通过利用该地区已有的资源，可以在很大程度上缓解该地区长期存在的饥饿问题。', 'G': '马拉维农业和粮食安全部长乌拉迪·穆萨坚称扩大小规模灌溉是当务之急，但马拉维缺乏技术和资金，无法独自实现；与此同时，南部非洲的慢性饥饿威胁着未来的一代，马拉维近一半的五岁以下儿童发育迟缓，无法上学，这些伤害将在雨季过后长期存在。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Once again, southern Africa is facing a severe food crisis. It is a chronic problem - and shouldn't be. At the Trinity hospital in Malawi's southern Nsanje district, three-year-old Mboyi is lying listless, his face against the wall. His belly is badly bloated and skin is peeling off his legs. His mother explains that the family has not been able to harvest anything this year, due to poor rains. Mothers in the area are already bringing malnourished children to hospitals in alarming numbers. Yet, it will be another six months before the next harvest.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Aid agencies are sounding the alarm, hoping that help will come before emaciated children's haunting images, such as those recently seen in Niger, appear on western television screens. The UN's World Food Programme (WFP) says that close to 12 million people across southern Africa will need food aid before the next harvest. The agency is short of more than $150 million to feed them over the next six months. Malawi and Zimbabwe are by far the hungriest, but Mozambique, Zambia, Lesotho, and Swaziland are also affected. The drought may be southern Africa's worst in a decade. The crucial rains of January, when newly planted crops need water, did not come on time. Nor, in some places, did seeds and fertiliser. Maize, the staple food, is scarce in some areas; prices in markets have shot up beyond the means of the hungriest.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Though the problem is particularly severe this year, it recurs across southern Africa. Food is produced mainly by subsistence farmers on small plots with no irrigation, their fate tied to rain falling in the right amount at the right time. Bad roads and unreliable transport make it expensive to move food and seeds. Without proper marketing channels, small farmers cannot sell whatever surplus they may have outside their neighbourhoods. This leaves southern Mozambicans hungry, even when crops are plentiful up north. Maize is ill-suited to the climate, needing too much water. In Malawi, there are too many people for the land. Partly due to bad farming, yields are low. And the region has the world's highest rate of AIDS.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Many small farmers struggle to make ends meet even in good years, so one bad season can be disastrous. And in Swaziland and Mozambique, they are facing their fourth dry year in a row. Unable to grow enough to feed themselves or borrow their way out of hard times, farmers end up losing the few assets they have. In Malawi, those without anything left often resort to cutting and selling firewood, further eroding the soil and making their plots still less productive, or else fishing already depleted waters. Others venture into crocodile-infested rivers to dig out water-lily tubers for food.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text:
Bad government policy sometimes makes things worse. In Zimbabwe, once the region's breadbasket, land grabs have crippled commercial agriculture and irrigation systems.
Hyperinflation and lack of foreign exchange make it hard to buy seeds and fertiliser, while fuel shortages stymie crop transport. A recent operation to "clean up" cities by bulldozing supposedly illegal dwellings has left another 700,000 people destitute, adding to the ranks of the hungry. The government has so far refused to endorse the UN's proposed emergency programme to help those affected. Other governments are less bloody-minded. Malawi, the worst-hit country, with 5 million people (nearly half its population) needing food handouts, wants help. In July, President Bingu wa Mutharika asked his compatriots to give to a "feed the nation" fund: so far, $565,000 has been collected. In August, the UN appealed for $88 million. The World Bank will give $30 million.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Harnessing what the region already has would go a long way to offsetting its chronic hunger. In southern Malawi, rivers regularly flood - and are badly managed. By contrast, a big sugar plantation in Nchalo, its sprinklers spitting out arcs of water, is a green oasis. On a smaller but no less hopeful scale, the nearby Chitsukwa irrigation scheme cost only about $20,000 and provided canals and enough low-tech pumps to water 18 hectares (45 acres), which sustain 176 farmers. Along the canal, women with babies on their backs labour on what look like portable stairmasters, pumping water into their fields: the maize is flourishing. Now armed with better knowledge, farmers are aiming at three crops a year, instead of the precarious single one to which they were accustomed. A few kilometres down the road, the land is hopelessly dry and barren.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Uladi Mussa, Malawi s minister for agriculture and food security, insists that expanding small-scale irrigation is a top priority. The potential is there, he explains, but Malawi lacks the know-how and money to do it on its own. Zambia and Mozambique have both welcomed exiled white Zimbabwean farmers, whose skills are already boosting local agriculture. Meanwhile, chronic hunger is threatening southern Africa's future generations. Close to half of Malawi's under-five-year-olds are stunted. Schools unable to feed their pupils report drops in attendance, as children are too weak to walk or are forced to help their parents find food. For them, the damage will remain long after the rains have come.

questions.title: The main reasons why there is a lack of food in southern Africa year after year
questions.analysis:
这道题要求我们找出导致南部非洲每年都缺粮的主要原因。我们可以通过对比题干中的关键词和原文中的同义表达来快速定位答案。

首先，题干中的关键词是"main reasons"，我们可以在原文中找到同义表达"subsistence farmers"，这个词出现在原文的第一句话中，即"Food is produced mainly by subsistence farmers on small plots with no irrigation, their fate tied to rain falling in the right amount at the right time."。

其次，题干中的关键词是"lack of food"，我们可以在原文中找到同义表达"Food is produced mainly by subsistence farmers on small plots"，这个词出现在原文的第一句话中，即"Food is produced mainly by subsistence farmers on small plots with no irrigation, their fate tied to rain falling in the right amount at the right time."。

最后，题干中的关键词是"southern Africa"，我们可以在原文中找到同义表达"it recurs across southern Africa"，这个词出现在原文的第一句话中，即"Though the problem is particularly severe this year, it recurs across southern Africa."。

综上所述，根据题干中的关键词和原文中的同义表达，我们可以得出结论，本题对应的段落是C段落。

questions.title: The things that some desperate farmers do to feed themselves and their families
questions.analysis:
题干中提到了一些关键词，如"desperate farmers"、"feed themselves and their families"。我们可以通过在原文中寻找这些关键词的同义表达来定位答案。

在原文中，"desperate farmers"的同义表达是"small farmers"，这个表达出现在第一段的句子中："Many small farmers struggle to make ends meet even in good years, so one bad season can be disastrous." 这段话提到了小农户在好年景中也很难维持生计，所以一旦遇到坏的季节，情况就会变得非常糟糕。

而"feed themselves and their families"的同义表达是"unable to grow enough to feed themselves"，这个表达出现在第二段的句子中："Unable to grow enough to feed themselves or borrow their way out of hard times, farmers end up losing the few assets they have." 这段话提到了农民无法种植足够的粮食来养活自己，也无法通过借贷来度过困难时期，最终导致他们失去了他们所拥有的一些财产。

content.title: Looking for Life on the Ocean Wave
content.summary: {'A': '一位企业家兼生物科学家在豪华游艇上利用细网捕捉地球上最丰富的微小生物，并研究其基因多样性，进而操控这些微生物的分子机制以生产清洁能源、新药物或增强地球“肺部”吸收更多二氧化碳的能力，从而限制全球变暖。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了美国科学家克雷格·文特尔的非凡努力，他成功识别了人类基因组，并因其大胆的方法而被称为“科学少年”。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了维恩特（Venter）在科学界遭到批评后仍然坚持不懈，成为第一个读取自己基因组的人，并致力于创造人工生物的合成基因组的非凡努力，同时他正在海洋DNA编码项目中，以商务与娱乐相结合的方式在美洲西海岸航行。他的帆船Sorcerer II是一个浮动实验室，通过获取海水中的任何物质的基因编码，得出了对海洋生命的全新观点，这是对查尔斯·达尔文19世纪在“比格尔”号上航行的现代回答。他称这种方法为“宏基因组学”。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了微生物在地球上占据了绝大部分的生命，并且在海洋中占据了生物质的90%。它们是生态系统中物质和能量的中央处理器，负责创造和维持我们呼吸的空气。它们也许是减缓全球变暖的最大希望，因为我们的海洋是碳的最大“汇”，部分原因是有机体从大气中吸收碳来构建它们的骨骼和壳，就像“肺”一样。令人惊讶的是，绝大多数这些有机体是未知的。“了解它们的角色和功能对于确保地球和人类生存至关重要，”位于马里兰州洛克维尔的J. Craig Venter研究所的创始人和主席Venter说道。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了索罗斯二号探险队在2003年开始的试点项目中，在百慕大附近的萨尔加索海发现了超过一百万个新基因，这被认为是海洋中的沙漠等价物。在接下来的两年里，文特尔来回飞行加入船员，从新斯科舍省的哈利法克斯到东热带太平洋取样水域。“我完成了所有重要的海洋航行。”他说。其中一次特别的航行，通过巴拿马运河，到科科斯岛，再到加拉帕戈斯群岛，“是一次转变的事件，非凡的”，他在探险中结合了基因组学、写自传和与鲨鱼潜水，所有这些都在探索频道的电视摄制组的注视下进行。', 'F': '维生素团队利用强大的计算能力重建和分析微生物DNA，发现了400种新微生物和600万个新基因，这些基因可以用来制造蛋白质，维生素公司希望利用这些基因信息将二氧化碳转化为丙烷等燃料，以及生产氢气作为清洁燃料。', 'G': '这段话主要讲述了海洋中的细菌病毒可能是导致某些海域碳吸收生物较少的原因，如果能够理解这种关系并找到抑制病毒或使细菌抗性的方法，将有更多的生物能够吸收二氧化碳。', 'H': '维恩特的项目对基础科学产生了最大的影响，推翻了关于生命之树的许多既定观念，揭示了所有海洋表面生物都能产生能够检测彩色光的蛋白质色素，这些蛋白质色素被发现是地球上最丰富和重要的基因家族之一，它们帮助微生物利用太阳能，但不通过光合作用，而是通过“光收集”机制将带电原子储存在太阳能电池中。', 'I': '这段话主要讲述了团队发现了许多保护微生物免受紫外线伤害的新蛋白质，以及一些修复紫外线损伤的蛋白质，并且他们惊讶地发现许多被认为只存在于某个生命界的蛋白质实际上更为广泛存在，这只是开始，“很明显，我们只是开始了解微生物世界的皮毛。”'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Put one buccaneering entrepreneur-cum-bioscientist on a luxury yacht. Using some mighty fine nets, let him trawl the world's oceans for the smallest creatures. Catalogue the genetic diversity of this, the most abundant form of life in the largest habitat on Earth. Then hijack the molecular machinery of these microbes to make clean energy, new drugs or boost the ability of the Earth's lungs to "breathe" more carbon dioxide, and so limit global warming.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: This may sound like the outline for a sci-fi potboiler, but it sums up the remarkable efforts of Craig Venter, the maverick American scientist. Seven wars ago. Venter announced at the White House that he had identified all the genes - the genome - in the DNA of a human being. It was the culmination of a bitter race with an international consortium of government labs, and his bull-in-a-china-shop approach earned him the epithet "the boy of science".

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: It did not deter him, and while many of the critics in the scientific establishment who vilified him disappeared from view. Venter went on to become the first person to read his own genome and is also undertaking an extraordinary effort to create a synthetic genome for an artificial organism. Today, however, he is bobbing in the middle of the Sea of Cortez, mixing business with pleasure in a project to read marine DNA codes as he sails along the west coast of the Americas. His 29-metre sloop, Sorcerer II, is a floating laboratory. Rather than use the traditional method of studying microorganisms by growing them in the lab, which only works with one species in every 100, Venter is obtaining the genetic codes of anything and everything present in sea water. The result is a radical new view of life in the oceans, the modem answer to Charles Darwin's I 9th-century voyage on the HMS Beagle. "We are starting to view the world in a gene-centred fashion," Venter says. "Our goal is to try to sort out evolution, working back from the genes to what organisms are there." He calls his approach"metagenomics".

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Microbes make up the vast majority of life on the planet and account for up to 90 per cent of the biological mass in the sea. They are the central processors of matter and energy in ecosystems. They are responsible for the creation and maintenance of the air we breathe. They are also, perhaps, our biggest hope of slowing global warming. Our oceans are the biggest"sink" of carbon, thanks in part to organisms that absorb carbon from the atmosphere to build their skeletons and shells, like "lungs". Remarkably, the vast majority of these organisms are unknown. "It is important to understand their role and function to ensure the survival of the planet and human life," says Venter, who is founder and chairman of the J. Craig Venter Institute in Rockville, Maryland.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The Sorcerer II expedition began with a pilot project in 2003 in the Sargasso Sea near Bermuda in which more than a million new genes were discovered in what was thought to be the marine equivalent of a desert. For the next two years, Venter flew back and forth to join the crew as it sampled the waters from Halifax, Nova Scotia, to the Eastern Tropical Pacific. 'I did all the major ocean passages." he said. One in particular, through the Panama Canal, up to Cocos Island and down to the Galapagos, "was a transforming event, phenomenal" as he combined genomics with writing an autobiography and diving with sharks, all under the gaze of a Discovery Channel TV crew.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Using phenomenal computing power to reconstruct and analyse microbial DNA, with a single stage of the calculations taking more than a million hours of supercomputer time, a flood of discoveries has come from the latest phase of the expedition. Venter announced in a trio of papers in PloS Biology a few days ago that his team had returned to port with 400 newly discovered microbes and six million new genes. Each gene contains the instructions used to make the proteins that build and operate living thing'-, and Waiter's bounty doubles the number known to science. His company, Synthetic Genomics, wants to harness this genetic information to use the microbes to turn carbon dioxide into propane and other fuels, short-circuiting the traditional geological process where ancient creatures are compressed into coal and oil over the aeons. Another target is hydrogen production, the ultimate clean fuel.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: When it conn's to climate change, the expedition has thrown up another key insight. Some parts of the ocean have more carbon-hungry organisms than others, and it used to he thought that populations reflected local nutrient levels. Venter has found that t his may not be the case.The culprit could be bacterial viruses phages - which keep microbe levels low in some seas. "If we can understand this relationship more, and find out how to inhibit the viruses, or make the bacteria resistant, we would have a lot more organisms capturing carbon dioxide," says Venter.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The biggest impact of his project has been on basic science, overturning many established ideas about the tree of life. It used to be thought that the protein pigment in our own eyes that enables us to detect light was rare. But Venter's gene trawl reveals that all surface marine organisms make proteorhodopsins that detect coloured light. "They turn out to be one of the most abundant and important, gene families on the planet," he said. Blue and green variants are found in different environments - blue light preferred in the open ocean such as the indigo Sargasso Sea and green light along coasts. Venter believes these proteins help microbes to use energy from the sun, as plants do, but without photosynthesis. Instead, they use this "light-harvesting" machinery to pump charged atoms in the equivalent of solar batteries.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: The team discovered many new proteins that protect microbes from UV rays and some that are involved in repairing the damage caused by UV. They were also surprised to discover that many kinds of protein that were thought to be specific to one kingdom of life were more widespread. This is only the start. "It's clear," says Venter, "that we've only begun to scratch the surface of understanding the microbial world."

questions.analysis:
要快速得到这个答案，我们可以按照以下步骤进行定位和答题：

1. 首先，我们需要找到与题干中的每个选项相关的信息。我们可以通过同义替换的方式来寻找这些信息。

2. 针对第一个选项 "Craig Venter is an unconventional scientist."，我们可以在原文中找到相关信息。原文中提到了一个科学家的名字 "Venter"，并且他的观点与传统观点不同。所以，我们可以得出结论，这个选项是正确的。

3. 接下来，我们来看第二个选项 "Carbon is used to make shells for sea creatures."。在原文中，我们找到了与之相关的信息。原文中提到了 "proteins that protect microbes from UV rays"，而这些蛋白质是用来保护微生物的。由于海洋生物中的贝壳是由碳酸盐构成的，所以我们可以推断出这个选项是正确的。

4. 第三个选项是 "The Sargasso Sea has long been thought of as not rich in life."。在原文中，我们找到了相关信息。原文中提到了 "we’ve only begun to scratch the surface of understanding the microbial world"，这意味着我们对微生物世界的了解还很有限。因此，我们可以推断出这个选项是正确的。

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: It can be hard to guess someone's exact age. A range of factors may leave marks on our appearance: how much sleep We've had - even the way we dress and our view of ourselves. The good news is that just as these factors can add years on to your appearance, it follows that they can also take years off. We don't always have control over some of those social factors that can make us look younger, but there are other steps we can take to try to stop the ravages of age.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: SOCIAL FACTORS

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Last month, the University of Southern Denmark published a report, The Influence of

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Environmental Factors on Facial Ageing, which showed that how we live can affect how old we look. In it, 1,826 twins were photographed and then ten female nurses aged between 25-46 years were asked to guess how old the "models" were. The results were intriguing. They showed that belonging to a high social class can make us look up to four years younger, and many other lifestyle factors were shown to affect the way we look. Having children was found to make men look a full year younger, though it had no effect on women, and having four or more children cancelled out the benefit.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Depression and sun exposure were the biggest factors in making you look old before your time. Depression added up to three and a half years to a woman's perceived age (and 2.4 years for men). Sun exposure piled on at least an extra year. Smoking put on six months for a woman and a year for a man. Meanwhile, having a high BMI (body mass index) was found to take a whole year off for both men and women. "If you are not depressed, not a smoker and not too skinny, you are basically doing well," says Professor Kaare Christensen (married, three children, non-smoker), one of the report's authors. Professor Christensen's report concluded that it was more dangerous for our health to look a year older, than to actually be a year older.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: NUTRITION

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: This is possibly the biggest change we can make fairly easily. There are four main factors that prematurely age us: smoking, too much alcohol, lack of fresh fruit and vegetables, and insufficient protein intake. You can immediately tell a smoker. It's not just the lines around the mouth and eyes, but smoking is dehydrating to the body. Every time you inhale on a cigarette,you're taking toxins into the body which have to be diffused and detoxified by the liver and kidneys, and they're dependent on plenty of fresh water to carry toxins away. Most smokers don't drink anywhere near enough water.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: The really big, quick fix, though, is eating more fresh fruit and vegetables. You can see if someone doesn't eat enough, or any, fresh fruit and veg in a minute. The skin lacks a freshness and translucency. This is because the skin is the last organ to benefit from the nutrients you eat- the likes of the brain, heart, and lungs all get first share. If someone's diet is lacking in fruit and veg, the skin will become dehydrated. This is a sign that sufficient nutrients aren't being delivered, so from an anti-ageing point of view, it's important to have live, fresh food and raw food is vital. If you have to cook, steaming will retain at least some of the vitamins and minerals.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: The other really important thing, and one we tend to miss out on in our diet-obsessed culture, is adequate intake of essential fatty acids (EFAs), from oily fish, nuts, and seeds. EFAs are vital for prolonging life expectancy because every cell in the body has a phospholipid bilayer that protects it, but they also give the skin a dewy, "bouncy", youthful feel. One of the worst things you can do in terms of looking old is to go on a low-fat diet. Stress is another big one for adding years. We can help support the adrenal and thyroid glands, which take a hammering when we're stressed, by eating plenty of fresh vitamin C and magnesium for the adrenal glands; and iodine, selenium, zinc, and B vitamins to support the thyroid.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: EXERCISE

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: We've come to think of exercise as a pure slimming pursuit and women tend to be rather scared of lifting weights, but building lean tissue through weight-bearing exercise is key to keeping the years at bay. Exercise can help reduce the effects of ageing by slowing down the decline of type II muscle fibres. Generally, type I muscle fibres deal with aerobic activities and type II with anaerobic ones. The type II responds to resistance work to improve muscle tone. With ageing, there's a reduction in frequency, duration, and intensity of habitual activity: we generally move less. So, these type II fibres deteriorate because they simply don't get enough stimuli.

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: SKIN CARE

paragraphs.label: M
paragraphs.text: Almost every skin cream promises to make you look younger. It's a promise many are seduced by, but many end up disappointed. The problem is not that products don't work, but starting too late, and then not spending enough money. A lot of people skip good skin care until they think they need it, and by then it's actually too late. In women, the skin around the eyes is the first to go, in men it's the hands. A good routine should start early because maintenance is much easier than repair.

paragraphs.label: N
paragraphs.text: Your skin also becomes more transparent as you get older, so you need to adapt your make-up and hair colour accordingly. Foundation should be lighter than you'd imagine, and sheerer, and if you want to cover grey, don't be tempted to go for a too-dark hair colour or block colour -highlights are kind. Don't forget to apply moisturiser around the back of the neck: It's the only bit of skin attached to a bone, so it's important that you look after it to avoid sagging.

题干问的是运动如何帮助人看起来更年轻。我们可以通过以下步骤快速找到答案：

1. 首先，我们注意到题干中的关键词是"exercise"和"look younger"。
2. 在原文中，我们可以找到与"exercise"相关的同义表达"weight-bearing exercise"，这在第一句话中提到："We’ve come to think of exercise as a pure slimming pursuit and women tend to be rather scared of lifting weights, but building lean tissue through weight-bearing exercise is key to keeping the years at bay."（我们已经将运动视为纯粹的减肥追求，女性往往对举重有些害怕，但通过负重运动来建立瘦肌肉组织是保持年轻的关键。）
3. 接下来，我们需要找到与"look younger"相关的信息。在原文中，我们可以找到与之相关的同义表达"reduce the effects of ageing"，这在第二句话中提到："Exercise can help reduce the effects of ageing by slowing down the decline of type II muscle fibres."（运动可以通过减缓II型肌纤维的衰退来减少衰老的影响。）
4. 综上所述，根据原文，我们可以得出答案："Exercise helps keep type II muscle fibres in better condition."（运动有助于保持II型肌纤维的良好状态。）
5. 因此，答案是B选项："It helps keep type II muscle fibres in better condition."（它有助于保持II型肌纤维的良好状态。）
